icp.label: Execution-stalled operator
icp.description: Founders, CEOs, or CROs at $2M-$50M revenue companies in Tech, Telecom, IT Services, or B2B SaaS who have a viable offer but are stuck in the gap between knowing what to do and actually doing it. They're not failing from lack of ideas — they're failing from lack of rhythm, structure, and follow-through.
trigger_set: Hiring sales team — posting for SDR, AE, VP Sales, CRO roles
New product or offer launch — expanding beyond core offering
Growth plateau — revenue flatlined after initial growth phase
Entering new market — geographic or vertical expansion
Funding event — just raised Series A/B/C, now under pressure to execute
signals: Job postings for sales/GTM roles — building or rebuilding revenue engine
Press releases about new product lines or market entry
Revenue plateau visible in public statements, interviews, or industry reports
Funding announcements with stated plans to 'scale GTM' or 'accelerate growth'
Leadership team changes — VP Sales or CRO departures
Podcast or interview where CEO admits to execution challenges

triggers.label: Moments of visible tension
sources: Press releases announcing pivots, relaunches, or 'new direction'
Crunchbase/funding data showing companies that raised but stalled on hiring
Job postings that signal panic-hiring (broad role descriptions, posted then removed)
Product Hunt launches with no follow-up traction (no updates, flat engagement)
Twitter/X threads revealing execution frustration or 'lessons learned from failure'
Substack/newsletter posts admitting to being stuck or overwhelmed
Indie Hackers / Hacker News posts asking for accountability or execution help
Google News for small companies announcing restructuring or pivots
Podcast appearances where founders admit to stalled growth

hiring_sales_team: B2B SaaS company hiring 'VP Sales' OR 'Head of Sales' OR 'CRO' 2026
tech company hiring 'SDR' OR 'Account Executive' OR 'sales team' first 2026
IT services company 'building sales team' OR 'first sales hire' 2026
new_product_offer: tech company 'launching new product' OR 'new offering' OR 'expanding portfolio' 2026
B2B SaaS 'new product launch' OR 'product expansion' CEO founder 2026
IT services 'new service line' OR 'new vertical' announcement 2026
growth_plateau: CEO OR founder 'growth plateau' OR 'stalled revenue' OR 'flatlined' tech 2026
B2B SaaS 'struggling to grow' OR 'revenue plateau' OR 'hit a ceiling' 2026
tech company CEO podcast 'execution challenges' OR 'scaling pain' 2026
entering_new_market: tech company 'entering new market' OR 'geographic expansion' OR 'new vertical' 2026
B2B company 'expanding into' OR 'entering' market CEO announcement 2026
funding_event: series A OR series B tech company raised funding 2026
site:crunchbase.com funding round SaaS IT services telecom 2026
B2B startup 'just raised' OR 'closed round' OR 'Series A' accelerate growth 2026

triggers.triangulation_method: Cross-reference: person shows tension signal publicly + company is $2M-$50M revenue in target industries + person holds Founder/CEO/CRO role. All 3 must match for HIGH confidence. Two of 3 = MEDIUM. One = LOW — research before outreach.
channel_exclusions: LinkedIn DMs — dead channel, 3 years no results

offer.name: GTM Breakdown Session
offer.outcome: Walk out with a prioritized execution plan — what to do this week, what to stop doing, and the daily rhythm to sustain it
offer.positioning: Not strategy. Not coaching. Operator-to-operator breakdown of what's actually blocking your revenue.

qualification.name: Execution Readiness Check
qualification.use_in: First outreach CTA, content posts, offer doc

diagnostic.name: GTM Execution Diagnostic Call
diagnostic.use_in: Follow-up after qualification call, warm leads ready for deeper conversation

outreach_angles.name: The pattern recognition angle
outreach_angles.template: Saw your post about [specific tension]. I work with operators who hit exactly this wall — the offer is solid but the daily execution stalls. Not pitching, but I broke this down for someone in a similar spot recently. Want me to share what we found?

outreach_angles.name: The rhythm angle
outreach_angles.template: Your [product/service] clearly has demand — the gap looks like it's between knowing what to do and having the operating rhythm to do it daily. I run GTM Breakdown Sessions specifically for operators in this spot. Would a 15-min convo be useful?

outreach_angles.name: The specificity angle
outreach_angles.template: I noticed [specific signal — hiring post, launch, pivot]. That usually means [what it signals]. I help operators at this exact inflection point build the daily system that turns their offer into consistent revenue. Happy to share how if relevant.

outreach_angles.name: The peer angle
outreach_angles.template: I've been where you are — good offer, real skills, but the daily machine isn't running. Built a system that fixed it for me and now I run it for others. Not a course, not coaching — a 90-minute breakdown that gives you the operating rhythm. Interested?

outreach_sequence.description: 4-stage sequence before any meeting ask. Never skip stages.

connect.label: Connect
connect.goal: Open a thread. Show you noticed something specific about them. No pitch, no ask.
rules: Reference one specific, visible signal — a post, announcement, job listing, interview
End with a statement, not a question — let curiosity do the work
Zero mention of your offer or services
connect.example: Saw your post about rebuilding the sales motion after the Series A. That inflection point — enough funding to move, not enough structure to move fast — is one of the most expensive gaps in B2B. Interesting spot to be in.

warm.label: Warm
warm.goal: Establish credibility and authority. Give something useful with no strings attached.
warm.tone: Operator sharing a real observation. Confident, not salesy.
rules: Share a specific insight, breakdown, or pattern you've seen
Make it directly relevant to their visible situation
Optional soft question at the end — but not a meeting ask
No links unless it's a genuinely useful resource
warm.example: Following on from my last note — the pattern I see most often at your stage: the pipeline looks fine on paper but the daily execution rhythm is broken. Deals stall because no one owns the follow-through cadence. Not a GTM strategy problem. An operating rhythm problem. Worth knowing before you hire your next AE.

discover.label: Discover
discover.goal: Understand their actual situation before proposing anything.
discover.tone: Curious. Diagnostic. Not leading them toward your offer.
rules: Ask one specific, open-ended question about their current situation
Question should be based on their signals — not generic
This stage only happens if they've engaged or responded to Warm
discover.example: Quick question based on what you shared — when you look at your pipeline right now, is the problem generating enough conversations, or converting the ones you have? I find the answer changes everything about what to fix first.

ask.label: Ask
ask.goal: Request a 15-min call now that you know their situation and they know your credibility.
ask.tone: Direct and specific. Not a generic 'would love to connect'.
rules: Reference what you've learned about their situation
Make the ask specific to the problem you've identified
Use the Execution Readiness Check link (15 min) — low commitment
One ask only — no alternatives or multiple CTAs
ask.example: Based on what you described — pipeline's there but conversion's broken — that's exactly the 90-minute breakdown I run. Not a strategy session, not a pitch. We'd identify the two or three constraints killing your conversion rate and you'd leave with a daily operating plan. 15 minutes to see if it's worth your time: https://calendly.com/barnes-lam/free-consultation-24-hour-business-sprint

message_rules: Never use templates verbatim — personalize every message to the specific person and their visible tension
Lead with what you observed, not what you sell
No pitch in the first message — start a diagnostic conversation
Reference something specific they said or did publicly
Never say 'I noticed you're struggling' — reframe as 'I see you're at an inflection point'
